commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Maintainers need to decide whether the same-value live proof plus stubbed rotated-key tests is enough for an auth-provider storage change; no automated repair can create live Claude credential rotation evidence.; Cleared: No concrete supply-chain or security-boundary regression was found in the two-file diff; cookie lifecycle proof remains an auth-provider merge concern. commands.review_summary:
The branch updates Claude Web usage fetching to observe successful Set-Cookie sessionKey headers, reuse renewed session keys within the same fetch, persist browser/cache-backed renewals, and add focused cookie-renewal regression tests.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Partly: source inspection shows current main ignores Set-Cookie renewal and the PR tests reproduce rotated renewal with stubs, but this review did not run live Claude probes and the PR body has not captured a live rotated sessionKey.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers need to decide whether the same-value live proof plus stubbed rotated-key tests is enough for an auth-provider storage change; no automated repair can create live Claude credential rotation evidence.

target.title: Renew Claude web cookies
target.body:
## Summary
- renew Claude Web session keys from successful `Set-Cookie: sessionKey=...` responses
- reuse a rotated session key for later requests in the same Web fetch
- track renewal for manual cookie fetches too, while keeping manual cookies read-only so they do not rewrite cached browser-derived state

## Root cause
Claude Web usage requests reused a cached `sessionKey` but ignored `Set-Cookie` renewal headers from claude.ai. Once the cached key aged out, later refreshes could keep sending stale credentials. The first patch fixed browser/cache-backed fetches, but the manual-cookie path still skipped the renewal tracker because persistence provenance was also controlling in-memory renewal tracking.

## Fix
- create the renewal tracker for every Claude Web fetch
- use the tracker's current session key for `/usage`, extra usage, and account calls
- keep `CookieHeaderCache.store` gated on `cacheSourceLabel`, so only browser/cache-backed cookies are persisted
- extend the manual-cookie regression test to assert that `/usage` receives the renewed key while the cached browser cookie remains unchanged

## Proof
`swift test --filter ClaudeWebCookieRenewalTests`:

```text
Suite ClaudeWebCookieRenewalTests passed after 0.025 seconds.
Test run with 2 tests in 1 suite passed after 0.025 seconds.
```

`make check`:

```text
SwiftFormat completed in 0.2s.
0/1100 files require formatting.
Done linting! Found 0 violations, 0 serious in 1099 files.
```

Redacted live Claude Web fetch on 2026-06-14 17:06 Pacific:

```text
[claude-web] Found sessionKey in Safari
[claude-web] Found session key (24 cookies)
[claude-web] Organizations API status: 200
[claude-web] Organization resolved
[claude-web] Usage API status: 200
[claude-web] Account API status: 200
Cookie cache stored provider=claude source=Safari
```

Redacted live header probe on 2026-06-14 17:06 Pacific:

```text
cache-source: Safari
initial-sessionKey-sha256-12: e42266eaa620
organizations-status: 200
organizations-set-cookie-count: 1
organizations-sessionKey-set-cookie: no
usage-request-sessionKey-sha256-12: e42266eaa620
usage-used-renewed-sessionKey: not-applicable-no-renewal
usage-status: 200
usage-set-cookie-count: 1
usage-sessionKey-set-cookie: no
```

Redacted renewal watcher proof on 2026-06-15 05:43 Pacific:

```text
renewal_observed source=Safari initial=e42266eaa620 observed_from=usage renewed=e42266eaa620 changed=no usage_status=200 used_renewed=no followup_status=200 followup_used_observed=yes
```

This shows claude.ai emitted a live `Set-Cookie: sessionKey=...` response from the usage endpoint, and a follow-up request using that observed sessionKey succeeded with HTTP 200. The emitted value matched the existing key, so this is same-value sessionKey renewal proof rather than rotation proof.

## Proof limitation
Live proof now shows claude.ai emitting `Set-Cookie: sessionKey=...` and accepting a follow-up request with the observed key. The observed value matched the initial key, so the live artifact proves same-value renewal and follow-up reuse, while rotated-value behavior remains covered by request-header regression tests.

Refs #1287
